title: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a canonical snapshot, not an exhaustive ledger of later additive queue updates
summary: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE.json was last updated on 2026-07-11 and marks M-001 completed, while additive queue-update records and later team commits continue to record M-010 and other work. Therefore queue selection and historical reconstruction must read the canonical snapshot together with additive operations/queue-updates records and recent commit evidence. Absence from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone cannot establish that a later item, handoff, or completed slice does not exist.

status_relations.observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is dated 2026-07-11 and contains M-001 as the only continuity-owned item, while the committed M-010 queue update and later cross-team queue commits exist after that snapshot.
status_relations.inferred: A run that consults only ACTIVE_QUEUE.json can misclassify current ownership, duplicate completed work, or report a false absence of later queue history.
status_relations.proposed: Build a deterministic queue-state materializer that folds the canonical snapshot and ordered additive queue-update records without mutating historical inputs.
status_relations.superseded: Any operational assumption that ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone is exhaustive current queue state is superseded for runs observed after additive queue-update records were introduced.
status_relations.unresolved: The complete operations/queue-updates directory was not enumerated in this run, so no exhaustive current queue materialization is claimed. Event-order uniqueness and cross-team collision handling remain unverified.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, operations/queue-updates/M-010-2026-07-13T1445-0400.json, and recent queue commits. Falsify this record if the canonical queue already incorporates all later additive updates, or if repository policy explicitly defines additive queue-update records as non-operative historical notes only.
